highlight.title: <cite index="2-14">Novas Arc CQRS Case Study: 40% Query Latency Reduction</cite>
highlight.summary: <cite index="2-10,2-11,2-14">CQRS separates read and write operations by using different models, making it particularly effective for systems with high read-to-write ratios like e-commerce platforms. A 2026 case study showed this pattern reduced query latency by 40% in a high-traffic retail application.</cite>

resources.title: Netflix Architecture Case Study: How Does the World's Largest Streamer Build for Scale?
resources.why_now: <cite index="12-11,18-1">Netflix operates over 1000 microservices and recently expanded backend modernization through zero-configuration service mesh in 2026, providing a current blueprint for massive-scale system architecture.</cite>

resources.title: Event-Driven Architecture (EDA): A Complete Introduction
resources.why_now: <cite index="24-5">Over 72% of global organizations now use event-driven architecture to power their apps, making it essential knowledge for understanding how modern systems handle real-time data and distributed communication.</cite>

resources.title: Hexagonal Architecture (Ports & Adapters) Practical Guide
resources.why_now: <cite index="32-1,32-2,31-3">With frameworks changing rapidly and AI reshaping codebases, hexagonal architecture provides the insurance policy against technical churn by keeping business logic isolated and changeable.</cite>

resources.title: Software Architecture Patterns That Scale in 2026
resources.why_now: <cite index="7-13,7-14">Written specifically for 2026 conditions where cloud computing is more accessible and user expectations are at new heights, making architectural decisions more critical than ever for handling millions of users.</cite>

claude_take: <cite index="3-24,3-25,3-26">Software architecture isn't just a technical decision—it's about your team, your business, and how you want to grow. The fanciest pattern will fail if your team can't maintain it or if it doesn't align with how your organization actually works.</cite> The real insight from 2026 is that successful architectures are those designed for change, not perfection.
